title: `quiver:lint-spec` Command
objective: Ship `npx create-quiver lint-spec` that verifies consistency between SPEC.md, STATUS.md, and `slice.json` files: titles match, status values match, hours sum correctly, and no dangling references remain.
description: Specs, STATUS dashboards, and slice contracts are edited by hand and drift over time. This command parses the three artifacts and reports every discrepancy. It exits 0 on clean specs and exits 1 with a structured list when it finds issues. It does not auto-fix; humans resolve discrepancies.
must: Check STATUS.md lists every slice directory that exists under `slices/`
Check STATUS.md does not list nonexistent slice directories
Check SPEC.md "Definition of Done" and "Slices" tables do not reference removed slices
Check the sum of `estimated_hours` equals the total declared in STATUS.md
Check that titles match across SPEC.md, STATUS.md, and `slice.json.title`
Check that slice `status` values match between STATUS.md and `slice.json.status`
Exit 0 when clean, exit 1 when any check fails (lists all findings)
Add a `docs/COMMANDS.md` row and update `README.md` npm scripts list
not_included: Validating slice JSON structure beyond the consistency checks (`check-slice` owns that)
Running `lint-spec` as part of `check-pr` (discuss after validation checkpoint)
acceptance: `npx create-quiver lint-spec` returns exit 0 on the current Quiver repo with no false positives
Deliberately renaming a slice title in STATUS.md causes exit 1 with a clear diff message
Deliberately deleting a slice directory without updating STATUS.md causes exit 1
`--spec <slug>` restricts lint scope to one spec
`--json` produces a structured list of findings
`docs/examples/lint-spec.md` exists with a sample clean output and a sample failing output
assumptions: SPEC.md "Slices" tables follow the current convention (| Slice | Title | Status | Spec |)
STATUS.md tables follow the current convention used in v14
Parsing Markdown tables with regex is sufficient; no full Markdown AST required
